basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Taḥrīm — “The Prohibition” — opens on a delicate reproof to the Prophet, who had forbidden to himself something lawful to please his wives. Qushayrī draws from it the sovereign rule: the duty of setting the right of God before all things, at all times. He reads in it a divine jealousy (ghayra) over the heart of His beloved: when a servant lodges another in his heart, God troubles the dwelling of that intimacy, in jealousy over that heart, until he returns wholly to his Lord. The sūra then calls to sincere repentance (tawba naṣūḥ) — which Qushayrī defines as that which is followed by no relapse, which you do not see as coming from yourself but from your Lord, and which makes you find bitterness at the memory of the fault where once you found rest in committing it. It commands the protection of one's own from the Fire through education, then sets, at the threshold of its close, four figures of women: the treacherous wives of Noah and Lot, counter-models; and, radiant models, Pharaoh's wife who begs “a house with You,” and Mary, the wholly devoted, into whom God breathed of His Spirit.
themes: the right of God before all
the divine jealousy over the heart
guarding one's own from the Fire

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكَ ۖ تَبْتَغِي مَرْضَاتَ أَزْوَاجِكَ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu lima tuḥarrimu mā aḥalla llāhu laka tabtaghī marḍāta azwājika wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O Prophet! Why do you forbid what God has made lawful to you, seeking to please your wives? Yet God is Forgiving, Compassionate!
themes: the prohibition one imposes on oneself
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the reproof bears on this, that he forbade to himself, out of regard for his wife's heart, what God had made lawful to him — and the indication is the duty of setting the right of God, exalted be He, before all things, at all times. He reports that the Prophet, when this verse came down, would often repeat: “My God, I seek refuge in You from anything that cuts me off from You.” Tenderness for creatures must never become a veil between the heart and its Lord.

mots.ar: لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكَ
mots.tr: lima tuḥarrimu mā aḥalla llāhu lak
mots.racine: ح ر م / ح ل ل
mots.sens: why do you forbid what God made lawful to you
mots.def: ḥarrama: to declare forbidden; aḥalla: to make lawful.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ر ض و / ز و ج
mots.sens: seeking the pleasure of your wives
mots.def: ibtaghā: to seek; marḍāt: pleasure, satisfaction.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: God is Forgiving, Compassionate

versets.ar: قَدْ فَرَضَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمَانِكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ مَوْلَاكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: qad faraḍa llāhu lakum taḥillata aymānikum wa-llāhu mawlākum wa-huwa l-ʿalīmu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: God has already ordained for you the absolution of your oaths, and God is your Master! He is the All-Knowing, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: God opens a way of release (taḥilla): a hasty oath is no prison. Qushayrī stresses the word mawlākum — God is your Master, your Ally and Protector: the One who reproves you is also the One who frees you. The reproof and the release come from the same hand, and this is the mark of the true Master: He corrects without abandoning.

mots.ar: قَدْ فَرَضَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمَانِكُمْ
mots.tr: qad faraḍa llāhu lakum taḥillata aymānikum
mots.racine: ف ر ض / ح ل ل / ي م ن
mots.sens: God has ordained the absolution of your oaths
mots.def: faraḍa: to ordain; taḥilla: release, expiation; aymān: oaths.

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.sens: God is your Master

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م
mots.sens: the All-Knowing, the Wise

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلَىٰ بَعْضِ أَزْوَاجِهِ حَدِيثًا فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَتْ بِهِ وَأَظْهَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ عَرَّفَ بَعْضَهُ وَأَعْرَضَ عَن بَعْضٍ ۖ فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَهَا بِهِ قَالَتْ مَنْ أَنبَأَكَ هَٰذَا ۖ قَالَ نَبَّأَنِيَ الْعَلِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-idh asarra n-nabiyyu ilā baʿḍi azwājihi ḥadīthan fa-lammā nabbaʾat bihi wa-aẓharahu llāhu ʿalayhi ʿarrafa baʿḍahu wa-aʿraḍa ʿan baʿḍin fa-lammā nabbaʾahā bihi qālat man anbaʾaka hādhā qāla nabbaʾaniya l-ʿalīmu l-khabīru
versets.trad: When the Prophet confided a secret to one of his wives, and she divulged it, God informed the Prophet of it; he then made known to her part of what he had learned and kept back the rest. When he spoke of it, she exclaimed: “Who informed you of this?” He replied: “The All-Knowing, the All-Aware informed me.”
versets.commentaire: The Prophet reveals one part and passes over the rest: Qushayrī sees in it a trait of nobility (karam) — he reproved her on one part and turned his eyes from the rest, in the manner of the noble. The generous who knows all does not say all: he blames without crushing, leaves a door open to repentance. And the ultimate Informer names Himself: al-ʿAlīm al-Khabīr, the One who knows and is aware of all — no secret of the heart escapes Him.

mots.ar: وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلَىٰ بَعْضِ أَزْوَاجِهِ حَدِيثًا
mots.tr: wa-idh asarra n-nabiyyu ilā baʿḍi azwājihi ḥadīthan
mots.racine: س ر ر / ز و ج / ح د ث
mots.sens: when the Prophet confided a saying to one of his wives
mots.def: asarra: to confide in secret; ḥadīth: saying.

mots.racine: ع ر ف / ع ر ض
mots.sens: he made known part and turned from part
mots.def: ʿarrafa: to make known; aʿraḍa: to turn away.

mots.racine: ن ب أ
mots.sens: who informed you of this?

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ع ل م / خ ب ر
mots.sens: the All-Knowing, the All-Aware informed me

versets.ar: إِن تَتُوبَا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَقَدْ صَغَتْ قُلُوبُكُمَا ۖ وَإِن تَظَاهَرَا عَلَيْهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ مَوْلَاهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصَالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ ظَهِيرٌ
versets.tr: in tatūbā ilā llāhi fa-qad ṣaghat qulūbukumā wa-in taẓāharā ʿalayhi fa-inna llāha huwa mawlāhu wa-jibrīlu wa-ṣāliḥu l-muʾminīna wa-l-malāʾikatu baʿda dhālika ẓahīrun
versets.trad: [O wives,] if you both turn back to God, it is because your hearts have inclined. But if you support one another against the Prophet, know that God is his protector, as are Gabriel and every righteous one among the believers, and the angels too support him.
themes: the heavenly support of the Prophet
versets.commentaire: The call to repentance (tawba): let the hearts incline (ṣaghat) once more toward God. Qushayrī: the reproof targets “the wandering of passing thoughts of the heart” — not a grave act, but the simple inner leaning that strays. And “the righteous among the believers” (ṣāliḥ al-muʾminīn), he clarifies, denotes one whose heart holds no hypocrisy. The Prophet is never alone: God, Gabriel, the righteous, and the angels form around him one same support (ẓahīr).

mots.racine: ت و ب
mots.sens: if you both turn back to God

mots.racine: ص غ و / ق ل ب
mots.sens: your hearts have inclined

mots.racine: ظ ه ر
mots.sens: if you band together against him
mots.def: taẓāhara: to support one another, league.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ظ ه ر
mots.sens: and the angels, beyond that, are a support

versets.ar: عَسَىٰ رَبُّهُ إِن طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَن يُبْدِلَهُ أَزْوَاجًا خَيْرًا مِّنكُنَّ مُسْلِمَاتٍ مُّؤْمِنَاتٍ قَانِتَاتٍ تَائِبَاتٍ عَابِدَاتٍ سَائِحَاتٍ ثَيِّبَاتٍ وَأَبْكَارًا
versets.tr: ʿasā rabbuhu in ṭallaqakunna an yubdilahu azwājan khayran minkunna muslimātin muʾminātin qānitātin tāʾibātin ʿābidātin sāʾiḥātin thayyibātin wa-abkāran
versets.trad: It may be that his Lord, if he divorces you, will give him in exchange wives better than you — submissive to God, believing, devout, repentant, worshipping, given to fasting, whether previously married or virgins.
themes: the portrait of the soul God loves
versets.commentaire: The verse lists seven qualities of the wife God loves: submissive (muslima), believing (muʾmina), devout (qānita), repentant (tāʾiba), worshipping (ʿābida), journeying for God — that is, fasting (sāʾiḥa). Qushayrī sees here the portrait of the accomplished soul: it is not the outward condition (previously married or virgin) that gives a being its worth, but these inner qualities. The threat of exchange is in truth an invitation to become that soul.

mots.racine: ع س ي
mots.sens: it may be that his Lord

mots.racine: ب د ل / ز و ج
mots.sens: to give him in exchange better wives

mots.racine: ق ن ت / ت و ب / ع ب د / س ي ح
mots.sens: devout, repentant, worshipping, fasting

mots.racine: ث ي ب / ب ك ر
mots.sens: previously married or virgins

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَهْلِيكُمْ نَارًا وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالْحِجَارَةُ عَلَيْهَا مَلَائِكَةٌ غِلَاظٌ شِدَادٌ لَّا يَعْصُونَ اللَّهَ مَا أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū qū anfusakum wa-ahlīkum nāran waqūduhā n-nāsu wa-l-ḥijāratu ʿalayhā malāʾikatun ghilāẓun shidādun lā yaʿṣūna llāha mā amarahum wa-yafʿalūna mā yuʾmarūna
versets.trad: O you who believe! Guard yourselves and your families against a Fire whose fuel is people and stones! Over it stand stern and mighty angels who do not disobey God in what He commands them, and who do what they are commanded.
themes: guarding one's own from the Fire
versets.commentaire: “Guard yourselves and your families”: Qushayrī opens a whole range of meanings — teach them, educate them, call them to the obedience of God, keep them from deserving punishment by your guidance and instruction; display upon yourselves the acts of worship so they may learn from you and grow accustomed to them; point them to the Path and the Community; teach them the fair virtues; bid them accept counsel. True protection is no raised wall but a transmitted light — first lived, then offered.

mots.racine: و ق ي / أ ه ل / ن و ر
mots.sens: guard yourselves and your families from a Fire
mots.def: waqā: to guard, protect; ahl: family.

mots.racine: و ق د / ح ج ر
mots.sens: its fuel: people and stones

mots.racine: م ل ك / غ ل ظ / ش د د
mots.sens: stern, mighty angels

mots.racine: ع ص ي / أ م ر
mots.sens: they disobey not what He commands

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَا تَعْتَذِرُوا الْيَوْمَ ۖ إِنَّمَا تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna kafarū lā taʿtadhirū l-yawma innamā tujzawna mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: O you who have denied, make no excuses today! You are only requited for what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the time has passed, the matter grows grave, the door shuts, the stratagems fall — the duty, then, is haste and flight [to God], to reach the breath of repose. The excuse (ʿudhr) has its hour: it avails as long as the respite of verse 8 (repentance) lasts, but on the Day when there is no more respite, only the bare truth of deeds remains.

mots.racine: ع ذ ر
mots.sens: make no excuses today

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ع م ل
mots.sens: you are requited only for your deeds

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَوْبَةً نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُكَفِّرَ عَنكُمْ سَيِّئَاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ يَوْمَ لَا يُخْزِي اللَّهُ النَّبِيَّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ ۖ نُورُهُمْ يَسْعَىٰ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَانِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū tūbū ilā llāhi tawbatan naṣūḥan ʿasā rabbukum an yukaffira ʿankum sayyiʾātikum wa-yudkhilakum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru yawma lā yukhzī llāhu n-nabiyya wa-lladhīna āmanū maʿahu nūruhum yasʿā bayna aydīhim wa-bi-aymānihim yaqūlūna rabbanā atmim lanā nūranā wa-ghfir lanā innaka ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: O you who believe, turn back to God in sincere repentance! Your Lord may well efface your evil deeds and admit you into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, on the Day when God will not disgrace the Prophet nor those who believed with him. Their light will run before them and on their right. They will say: “Our Lord, perfect for us our light and forgive us! Truly, You are powerful over all things.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives three definitions of sincere repentance (tawba naṣūḥ): that which no relapse follows; that which you do not see as coming from yourself, nor attribute your salvation to yourself, but see as coming from your Lord; that which makes you find bitterness in the heart at the memory of the fault, as you once found rest in committing it. On “their light runs before them,” he says: this expresses that faith radiates from all their directions — light of tawḥīd, light of gnosis, light of faith. And their prayer “perfect our light” is the humble perseverance in supplication, for none is ever quit of asking.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ن ص ح
mots.sens: turn to God in sincere repentance

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
mots.sens: that He efface your evil deeds

mots.racine: ن و ر / س ع ي
mots.sens: their light runs before them
mots.def: nūr: light; saʿā: to run, hasten.

mots.racine: ت م م / ن و ر
mots.sens: Lord, perfect our light

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جَاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ وَاغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَمَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nabiyyu jāhidi l-kuffāra wa-l-munāfiqīna wa-ghluẓ ʿalayhim wa-maʾwāhum jahannamu wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: O Prophet! Strive against the deniers and the hypocrites; be firm toward them. Their refuge will be Hell: what a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī tempers the harshness by the order of times: He had first commanded him gentleness, in the time of the call — “dispute with them in the fairest way”; then, when they obstinately persisted after the clarity of the proof, He said: “be firm toward them.” Firmness is not the first movement but the last: it comes only after excuses are exhausted, when gentleness has offered all in vain.

mots.racine: ج ه د / ك ف ر / ن ف ق
mots.sens: strive against the deniers and hypocrites

mots.racine: غ ل ظ
mots.sens: be firm toward them

mots.racine: أ و ي
mots.sens: their refuge is Hell

mots.racine: ب أ س / ص ي ر
mots.sens: what a wretched end
mots.def: biʾsa: verb of blame; maṣīr: end.

versets.ar: ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا امْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَامْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ ۖ كَانَتَا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا صَالِحَيْنِ فَخَانَتَاهُمَا فَلَمْ يُغْنِيَا عَنْهُمَا مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَقِيلَ ادْخُلَا النَّارَ مَعَ الدَّاخِلِينَ
versets.tr: ḍaraba llāhu mathalan lilladhīna kafarū mraʾata nūḥin wa-mraʾata lūṭin kānatā taḥta ʿabdayni min ʿibādinā ṣāliḥayni fa-khānatāhumā fa-lam yughniyā ʿanhumā mina llāhi shayʾan wa-qīla dkhulā n-nāra maʿa d-dākhilīna
versets.trad: God has set forth as an example to those who deny the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. Both were under two righteous men among Our servants; they betrayed them, but it availed them nothing against God. It was said: “Enter the Fire with those who enter.”
themes: the nearness that does not save
versets.commentaire: Two wives of prophets, lost despite the highest companionship. Qushayrī: because separation had been decreed for them on the day of the [antecedent] allotment, nearness availed them nothing on the day of punishment. The holiest outward bond — to be the wife of Noah, of Lot — does not redeem a heart that betrays. What saves is not the nearness of bodies but the faithfulness of the inner; a lesson the following verses will turn into light.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.sens: God set forth an example
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to give a parable; mathal: example.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ص ل ح
mots.sens: they were under two righteous servants

mots.racine: خ و ن
mots.sens: they betrayed them

mots.racine: د خ ل / ن و ر
mots.sens: enter the Fire with those who enter

versets.ar: وَضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا امْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ إِذْ قَالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِندَكَ بَيْتًا فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِي مِن فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِ وَنَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ḍaraba llāhu mathalan lilladhīna āmanū mraʾata firʿawna idh qālat rabbi bni lī ʿindaka baytan fī l-jannati wa-najjinī min firʿawna wa-ʿamalihi wa-najjinī mina l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: And God has set forth as an example to the believers the wife of Pharaoh, when she said: “My Lord! Build for me, with You, a house in Paradise, and save me from Pharaoh and his doings! Save me from the unjust people!”;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defends the greatness of her request against any who would deem it small: some said her aspiration was small, in asking only for a house in Paradise, when she might have asked for more. But no: she said “build for me with You” — she first sought the neighbourhood of nearness (ʿindiyya), and a house in that neighbourhood is better than a thousand palaces outside it. She asks for the Neighbour before the dwelling: her heart aimed at the “with You” more than at the “Garden.”

mots.ar: رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِندَكَ بَيْتًا فِي الْجَنَّةِ
mots.tr: rabbi bni lī ʿindaka baytan fī l-janna
mots.racine: ب ن ي / ج ن ن
mots.sens: Lord, build for me with You a house in Paradise
mots.def: banā: to build; ʿinda: with, beside.

mots.racine: ن ج و / ع م ل
mots.sens: save me from Pharaoh and his deeds

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.sens: from the unjust people

versets.ar: وَمَرْيَمَ ابْنَتَ عِمْرَانَ الَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَهَا فَنَفَخْنَا فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِنَا وَصَدَّقَتْ بِكَلِمَاتِ رَبِّهَا وَكُتُبِهِ وَكَانَتْ مِنَ الْقَانِتِينَ
versets.tr: wa-maryama bnata ʿimrāna llatī aḥṣanat farjahā fa-nafakhnā fīhi min rūḥinā wa-ṣaddaqat bi-kalimāti rabbihā wa-kutubihi wa-kānat mina l-qānitīna
versets.trad: and Mary, daughter of ʿImrān, who kept her body chaste, into which We breathed of Our Spirit; she affirmed the truth of the words of her Lord and of His Books, and she was among the devout.
versets.commentaire: The sūra, so full of female figures, is sealed upon Mary (Maryam): Qushayrī notes that God willed not to leave the sūra without the mention of Mary, in distinction for her rank, after Pharaoh's wife. She is pure preservation (aḥṣanat), the one into whom God breathed of His Spirit, the one who believed truthfully (ṣaddaqat) in the words and Books of her Lord, and who remained among the constant devout (qānitīn). From the prohibition of the first verse to the gift of the Spirit in the last, the sūra ends on the face of the wholly devoted soul.

mots.racine: ح ص ن / ف ر ج
mots.sens: who kept her chastity

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ر و ح
mots.sens: We breathed of Our Spirit
mots.def: nafakha: to breathe in; rūḥ: spirit.

mots.racine: ص د ق / ك ل م / ك ت ب
mots.sens: she affirmed her Lord's words and Books
mots.def: ṣaddaqa: to affirm as true; kalimāt: words; kutub: Books.

mots.racine: ق ن ت
mots.sens: she was among the devout
